warning: All entries in this file are AI-generated guidance. They are NOT regulatory text. Verify against official CCC-2:2024 before use in formal audits.

audit_questions: Has the Authorizing Official formally approved a document listing all cloud services provided, including service type and data classification?
Is there a documented approval record showing AO sign-off on cybersecurity requirements per cloud service category?
Does the entity have a defined process for AO approval of new cloud service offerings before launch?
suggested_evidence: Signed authorization memo or board resolution covering cloud service scope
Cloud service registry with AO approval signatures and data classification
New service approval checklist with AO sign-off records
indicators_of_implementation: Formal document listing cloud services with AO approval date
Evidence of risk thresholds documented per service category
Traceable approval trail from AO for each active cloud service
red_flags: No formal AO authorization document exists
Cloud services offered without recorded AO approval
Risk thresholds are undefined or not approved

maturity_signals.initial: No formal authorization process; cloud services launched ad hoc.
maturity_signals.developing: AO approval sought informally for some services; no consistent documentation.
maturity_signals.defined: Documented authorization process; AO approves new cloud services before launch.
maturity_signals.managed: Centralized cloud service registry with AO approval records; periodic review scheduled.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Automated workflow enforces AO approval gates; registry auto-updated; risk thresholds reviewed quarterly.

audit_questions: Does the cloud service registry include service type (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS) and data classification for each service?
suggested_evidence: Cloud service inventory document with service type and classification columns
indicators_of_implementation: Inventory is current and classified
red_flags: Inventory missing or lacks data classification

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity requirements documented per cloud service category and formally approved?
suggested_evidence: Security requirements document signed by AO
indicators_of_implementation: Requirements exist for each service category
red_flags: Requirements generic or not service-specific

audit_questions: Are residual risk acceptance decisions formally documented and approved by the AO?
suggested_evidence: Risk acceptance records with AO signature
indicators_of_implementation: Residual risk levels defined per service
red_flags: No formal risk acceptance documentation

audit_questions: Are roles and responsibilities for cloud cybersecurity management formally assigned and documented?
suggested_evidence: RACI matrix or role description documents
indicators_of_implementation: Named owners for each cloud cybersecurity function
red_flags: Roles undefined or overlapping

audit_questions: Is there a documented approval gate that must be passed before launching a new cloud service?
suggested_evidence: New service approval form with defined criteria
indicators_of_implementation: No service launched without approval gate completion
red_flags: Services launched without formal approval

audit_questions: Has the Authorizing Official formally approved the use of each cloud service before adoption?
Is there a documented record of AO approval for each cloud service currently in use?
suggested_evidence: Signed cloud service adoption approval memo or decision record
Cloud service register with AO approval dates
indicators_of_implementation: No cloud service in active use without prior AO approval
Approval records accessible to auditors
red_flags: Cloud services adopted without formal approval
No evidence of AO involvement in cloud adoption decisions

maturity_signals.initial: Cloud services adopted without formal governance.
maturity_signals.developing: Informal approval sought but not consistently documented.
maturity_signals.defined: AO approval required; approval records maintained.
maturity_signals.managed: Centralized register with AO approvals; periodic review.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Automated approval workflow with AO digital sign-off.

audit_questions: Is there a formally established cloud cybersecurity function or team with defined scope and reporting line?
Are roles and responsibilities for cloud cybersecurity management documented and communicated?
suggested_evidence: Organizational chart showing cloud cybersecurity function
Job descriptions for cloud cybersecurity roles
Governance framework document for cloud cybersecurity
indicators_of_implementation: Named cloud cybersecurity team or function exists
Reporting line and authority are defined
Function is operationally active
red_flags: No dedicated cloud cybersecurity function
Cloud security managed ad hoc by general IT staff

maturity_signals.developing: Responsibilities assigned informally to existing staff.
maturity_signals.defined: Dedicated function established with documented scope.
maturity_signals.managed: Function has KPIs, budget, and reports to senior management.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Mature function with continuous improvement program and board reporting.

audit_questions: Is a responsible party formally designated for cloud cybersecurity within the entity?
Does the designated party have sufficient authority and resources?
suggested_evidence: Appointment letter or role description for cloud cybersecurity owner
Evidence of budget or resource allocation
indicators_of_implementation: Named individual accountable for cloud cybersecurity
Authority formally granted in writing
red_flags: No named responsible party for cloud cybersecurity
Responsible party lacks authority to enforce controls

maturity_signals.defined: Formally appointed with documented authority.
maturity_signals.managed: Accountable with regular reporting to management.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Integrated into enterprise governance with measurable objectives.

audit_questions: Do cloud-specific cybersecurity policies exist and are they formally approved?
Do the policies cover infrastructure security, tenant data protection, and multi-tenancy requirements?
Were policies reviewed in the past 12 months or after a significant change?
suggested_evidence: Approved cloud cybersecurity policy document with version history
Review log showing annual review completion
Distribution records proving policy communication to staff
indicators_of_implementation: Cloud-specific policy distinct from generic IT policy
Policy covers key cloud topics: multi-tenancy, data segregation, provisioning
Policy review cycle is documented and followed
red_flags: No cloud-specific cybersecurity policy
Generic IT policy applied to cloud without adaptation
Policy last reviewed more than 12 months ago

maturity_signals.developing: Draft policy exists but not formally approved.
maturity_signals.defined: Approved policy covering key cloud topics; annually reviewed.
maturity_signals.managed: Policy mapped to controls; compliance monitored.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Policies automatically triggered for review on change events; integrated with GRC.

audit_questions: Does the entity have a policy for secure use of cloud services?
Does it cover data classification, access control, acceptable use, and exit procedures?
suggested_evidence: Cloud acceptable use policy document
Data classification policy referencing cloud environments
indicators_of_implementation: Policy includes cloud-specific sections
Exit procedures are defined
red_flags: No policy for cloud service use
Exit procedures not documented

maturity_signals.defined: Approved policy covering all required topics.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Policy auto-reviewed on cloud service changes.

audit_questions: Does a cloud-specific risk register exist covering multi-tenancy and supply chain threats?
Are risk treatment plans documented with timelines and owners?
Is the risk register reviewed annually?
suggested_evidence: Cloud cybersecurity risk register
Risk treatment plan with owner assignments and target dates
Evidence of annual review and AO sign-off
indicators_of_implementation: Cloud-specific threat scenarios included
Treatment plans actionable and tracked
Residual risks formally accepted by AO
red_flags: Cloud risks not separately tracked
Risk register not reviewed in over 12 months
No treatment plans for high-rated risks

maturity_signals.defined: Documented risk register with treatment plans.
maturity_signals.managed: Risk register integrated with GRC; regular reviews.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Automated risk monitoring with real-time threat intelligence integration.

audit_questions: Are multi-tenancy and shared resource risks explicitly documented in the risk register?
suggested_evidence: Risk register entries for multi-tenancy and supply chain scenarios
indicators_of_implementation: Cloud-specific threats documented
red_flags: Risk register lacks cloud-specific scenarios

audit_questions: Are CIA risks for tenant data assessed and rated in the risk register?
suggested_evidence: Risk assessments covering data confidentiality, integrity, availability for each service
indicators_of_implementation: CIA triad explicitly assessed per cloud service
red_flags: Risk assessment does not distinguish CIA impacts

audit_questions: Do treatment plans include named owners and target completion dates?
suggested_evidence: Risk treatment plan tracker with owners and dates
indicators_of_implementation: All high/critical risks have active treatment plans
red_flags: Treatment plans lack ownership or deadlines

audit_questions: Are residual risk acceptance decisions formally signed by the AO?
suggested_evidence: Signed risk acceptance records per residual risk
indicators_of_implementation: Unmitigated risks formally accepted rather than ignored
red_flags: Residual risks not formally reviewed or accepted

audit_questions: Is there evidence of annual risk register review with dates and outcomes documented?
suggested_evidence: Annual review meeting minutes or risk review report
indicators_of_implementation: Review cycle is followed consistently
red_flags: No evidence of annual review

audit_questions: Does the CSP have a defined process for notifying tenants of significant risks or incidents?
Are notification timelines and channels documented?
suggested_evidence: Tenant notification procedure document
Sample incident notification sent to tenants
indicators_of_implementation: Notification templates exist
Tenants can demonstrate receiving past notifications
red_flags: No tenant notification process
Incidents discovered by tenants before CSP notifies them

maturity_signals.defined: Documented process with defined timelines.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Automated tenant notification with SLA enforcement.

audit_questions: Has the entity assessed the CSP's security posture and compliance with applicable Saudi regulations?
Are data residency and cross-border transfer risks formally assessed?
Is there a documented exit strategy for critical cloud services?
suggested_evidence: CSP security assessment report or third-party audit report (e.g., IRAP, ISO 27001)
Data residency confirmation from CSP
Cloud exit plan document
indicators_of_implementation: CSP assessment completed before and periodically after adoption
Data residency confirmed in writing
Exit plan tested or reviewed
red_flags: No CSP security assessment performed
Data residency unknown or unconfirmed
No exit plan exists

maturity_signals.defined: Structured CSP assessment covering all subcontrols.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Continuous CSP risk monitoring with automated alerts.

audit_questions: Is there a formal report documenting the CSP's security certifications and Saudi regulatory compliance?
suggested_evidence: CSP audit report, ISO 27001 certificate, or NCA compliance report
indicators_of_implementation: Assessment covers relevant certifications
red_flags: No documented CSP assessment

audit_questions: Has the entity confirmed data residency with the CSP and assessed cross-border transfer risks?
suggested_evidence: CSP contractual data residency clause
Data flow diagram showing where data is stored
indicators_of_implementation: Data residency clause in contract
Data flow diagram reviewed
red_flags: Data residency unconfirmed or data stored outside approved jurisdictions

audit_questions: Is there a documented exit plan and has data portability been confirmed with the CSP?
suggested_evidence: Cloud exit plan document
CSP data export capabilities documentation
indicators_of_implementation: Exit plan is documented and periodically reviewed
red_flags: No exit plan; vendor lock-in risk not assessed

audit_questions: Is there evidence of an annual compliance assessment against applicable NCA cloud regulations?
Are compliance gaps tracked with remediation timelines?
When was the last independent audit conducted and what were the findings?
suggested_evidence: Annual compliance assessment report against NCA/CCC-2
Compliance gap tracker with owners and dates
Independent audit report and management response
indicators_of_implementation: Assessment cycle is annual and documented
Gap tracker is actively maintained
Auditor independence is verifiable
red_flags: No annual compliance assessment
Compliance gaps tracked but not remediated
Last independent audit older than 24 months

maturity_signals.defined: Annual assessment with documented gaps.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Continuous compliance monitoring with automated reporting.

audit_questions: Does the assessment explicitly cover NCA CCC-2 requirements?
suggested_evidence: Assessment checklist mapped to CCC-2 controls
indicators_of_implementation: All CCC-2 controls covered in assessment
red_flags: Assessment does not reference CCC-2

audit_questions: Is there a documented gap remediation process with owners and target dates?
suggested_evidence: Gap register with assigned owners and target dates
indicators_of_implementation: All gaps have owners and timelines
red_flags: Gaps identified but not tracked to closure

audit_questions: Is the audit conducted by an independent and qualified auditor?
suggested_evidence: Auditor credentials and independence declaration
Audit engagement letter
indicators_of_implementation: Auditor has no conflict of interest
Audit scope covers cloud services
red_flags: Audit conducted by internal team without independence
Auditor not qualified in cloud security

audit_questions: Is a complete and current cloud asset inventory maintained?
Does the inventory include classification by sensitivity and criticality?
How frequently is the inventory reconciled against actual deployed assets?
suggested_evidence: Cloud asset inventory spreadsheet or CMDB export
Data classification labels in inventory
Reconciliation report showing last comparison date
indicators_of_implementation: Inventory covers all asset categories (compute, storage, network, API)
Classification levels match data sensitivity
Undiscovered assets detected by automated scan
red_flags: Inventory incomplete or not maintained
No classification in inventory
Inventory not reconciled in past 90 days

maturity_signals.defined: Comprehensive inventory covering all asset types.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Real-time inventory with automated drift detection and alerting.

audit_questions: Does the inventory include all compute assets: physical servers, VMs, and containers?
suggested_evidence: Inventory extract showing compute assets
indicators_of_implementation: Compute assets listed with classification
red_flags: Containers or VMs missing from inventory

audit_questions: Are storage systems, databases, and data repositories included in the inventory?
suggested_evidence: Inventory section covering storage assets
indicators_of_implementation: Storage assets with data classification
red_flags: Databases not tracked in inventory

audit_questions: Are network components such as virtual networks and load balancers inventoried?
suggested_evidence: Network asset section in inventory
indicators_of_implementation: Virtual network components tracked
red_flags: Network components absent from inventory

audit_questions: Are APIs, microservices, and cloud-native components catalogued?
suggested_evidence: API registry or service mesh inventory
indicators_of_implementation: APIs tracked with ownership
red_flags: No API inventory maintained

audit_questions: Are assets classified by sensitivity and criticality?
suggested_evidence: Classification column in inventory
indicators_of_implementation: All assets have a classification level
red_flags: Classification missing for any asset category

audit_questions: Is an automated asset discovery tool in place?
How are unauthorized assets detected and responded to?
suggested_evidence: Automated discovery tool configuration and scan reports
Alert and response procedure for unauthorized assets
indicators_of_implementation: Tool is actively scanning at defined frequency
Alerts generated for undiscovered assets
red_flags: No automated discovery; manual only
No process to act on unauthorized asset alerts

maturity_signals.defined: Automated discovery with unauthorized asset alerting.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Self-healing inventory with automated quarantine of unauthorized assets.

audit_questions: Does the CST maintain an inventory of all cloud services in use?
Does the inventory include the data classification of information processed by each service?
suggested_evidence: Cloud service inventory with provider, type, data classification, and owners
Data classification policy referencing cloud services
indicators_of_implementation: All cloud services inventoried before use
Business and technical owners identified
red_flags: Shadow cloud services not inventoried
Data classification unknown for any cloud service

maturity_signals.defined: Comprehensive inventory with classification.
maturity_signals.managed: Inventory integrated with procurement; auto-updated on subscription.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Real-time cloud spend and inventory monitoring with automated classification.

audit_questions: Does the inventory include service type and data classification for each cloud service?
suggested_evidence: Inventory with service type and data classification columns
indicators_of_implementation: All services classified
red_flags: Classification missing

audit_questions: Are business and technical owners named for each cloud service?
suggested_evidence: Ownership column in cloud service inventory
indicators_of_implementation: Named owners for all services
red_flags: Orphaned cloud services with no owner

audit_questions: Is data residency location documented for each cloud service?
suggested_evidence: Data residency column in inventory with country/region
indicators_of_implementation: Residency confirmed for all services storing classified data
red_flags: Residency unknown or outside approved jurisdictions

audit_questions: Is MFA enforced on all cloud management interfaces and control planes?
Are privileged sessions recorded and monitored?
When were privileged access reviews last conducted?
Is JIT access implemented for administrative accounts?
suggested_evidence: MFA enforcement policy and technical evidence (IdP config, login reports)
Privileged session recording configuration and sample recordings
Access review reports (quarterly) with approver sign-off
JIT access provisioning configuration and approval logs
indicators_of_implementation: Zero login possible to management interfaces without MFA
Session recordings retained per policy
No over-provisioned accounts found in access review
JIT requests logged and automatically expire
red_flags: MFA disabled for any privileged account
No session recording for administrative access
Access reviews not conducted in past quarter
Shared admin accounts in use

maturity_signals.initial: Basic username/password; no MFA; no access reviews.
maturity_signals.developing: MFA for some accounts; manual access reviews annually.
maturity_signals.defined: MFA enforced on all privileged accounts; quarterly access reviews; sessions logged.
maturity_signals.managed: JIT access implemented; automated deprovisioning; RBAC enforced; monthly review.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Zero-trust model; continuous access analytics; anomaly-based deprovisioning; real-time session monitoring.

audit_questions: Is MFA technically enforced — not just required by policy — on all cloud management interfaces?
suggested_evidence: IdP/SSO configuration showing MFA enforcement
Login audit log with no MFA bypass
indicators_of_implementation: MFA cannot be bypassed by any admin account
red_flags: MFA enforced by policy only, not technically

audit_questions: Are PAWs or equivalent secure access mechanisms deployed for cloud admin tasks?
suggested_evidence: PAW deployment records; network isolation evidence
indicators_of_implementation: Admin tasks only possible from designated secure endpoints
red_flags: Admins use personal or shared workstations

audit_questions: Is JIT access implemented with automatic expiry for privileged accounts?
suggested_evidence: JIT access system configuration; sample request and expiry logs
indicators_of_implementation: Privileged access automatically revoked after session ends
red_flags: Standing privileged access with no time-based expiry

audit_questions: Are privileged access sessions to cloud management systems recorded?
suggested_evidence: PAM session recording tool; sample recording
indicators_of_implementation: All privileged sessions recorded and stored
red_flags: Session recording disabled or not retained

audit_questions: Is there an automated deprovisioning process triggered by HR termination or role change events?
suggested_evidence: HR-to-IAM integration configuration; termination deprovisioning log
indicators_of_implementation: Accounts disabled within 1 business day of termination
red_flags: Terminated user accounts found still active

audit_questions: Were privileged access reviews conducted at least quarterly with documented approvals?
suggested_evidence: Quarterly access review reports with approver signatures
indicators_of_implementation: Reviews completed on schedule; removals actioned promptly
red_flags: Reviews not conducted or findings not actioned

audit_questions: Are administrative duties segregated to prevent any single person from performing conflicting cloud actions?
suggested_evidence: RBAC policy; access matrix showing segregation
indicators_of_implementation: No single account can both approve and execute critical changes
red_flags: Single admin account with unrestricted access to all functions

audit_questions: Is least privilege enforced — no account has more permissions than required for its role?
suggested_evidence: Access rights review showing least privilege alignment
indicators_of_implementation: Over-privileged accounts identified and remediated in reviews
red_flags: Accounts with admin rights not requiring them

audit_questions: Is RBAC implemented and aligned to job function definitions?
suggested_evidence: RBAC policy; role-to-job-function mapping document
indicators_of_implementation: Roles match job functions; no custom permissions outside role definitions
red_flags: Permissions assigned individually outside RBAC framework

audit_questions: Is there an audit trail for all access provisioning and deprovisioning activities?
suggested_evidence: IAM audit log showing provisioning events
indicators_of_implementation: All provisioning actions logged with timestamp and approver
red_flags: Access changes not logged

audit_questions: Is sharing of administrative credentials technically prevented?
suggested_evidence: Policy prohibiting shared accounts; individual account provisioning for all admins
indicators_of_implementation: All admin accounts are personal and non-shared
red_flags: Generic or shared admin accounts in use

audit_questions: Do cloud account password policies meet NCA requirements for length, complexity, and rotation?
suggested_evidence: Password policy configuration in IdP
Comparison against NCA password requirements
indicators_of_implementation: Policy enforced technically, not just by procedure
red_flags: Password policy below NCA minimum requirements

audit_questions: Is federated identity management implemented for cloud services?
Are only approved authentication protocols in use (e.g., SAML 2.0, OIDC)?
suggested_evidence: SSO/federation configuration
Protocol configuration showing approved standards
indicators_of_implementation: Users authenticate via central IdP
No legacy authentication protocols in use
red_flags: Multiple separate identity silos
Legacy protocols (NTLM, basic auth) in use for cloud access

maturity_signals.defined: Full federation across cloud services with approved protocols.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Passwordless authentication; continuous identity analytics.

audit_questions: Is MFA enforced on all accounts with admin access to cloud consoles?
Are access reviews for cloud accounts conducted at least semi-annually?
suggested_evidence: MFA enforcement evidence for cloud console access
Semi-annual access review reports
indicators_of_implementation: No admin account accessible without MFA
Review cycle is documented and followed
red_flags: MFA not enforced for any cloud console access
Access reviews not conducted or not documented

maturity_signals.defined: MFA required for all admins; semi-annual reviews.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Continuous access analytics; anomaly-based revocation.

audit_questions: Is MFA technically enforced for cloud console access?
suggested_evidence: IdP config or SSO policy showing MFA requirement
indicators_of_implementation: MFA cannot be bypassed
red_flags: MFA optional or bypass available

audit_questions: Were cloud service access reviews completed semi-annually?
suggested_evidence: Access review reports with dates and approver
indicators_of_implementation: Reviews on schedule; removals actioned
red_flags: Reviews overdue or findings not actioned

audit_questions: Are cloud accounts promptly deprovisioned on user departure or role change?
suggested_evidence: Sample deprovisioning tickets with timestamps
indicators_of_implementation: Deprovisioning within 1 business day
red_flags: Departed users with active cloud accounts

audit_questions: Are least-privilege principles applied to all cloud service accounts?
Is sharing of cloud service admin accounts prohibited and technically prevented?
suggested_evidence: Access rights review demonstrating least privilege
Policy prohibiting shared accounts; individual account logs
indicators_of_implementation: No account has more access than role requires
No shared accounts discovered
red_flags: Over-privileged accounts identified and not remediated
Shared cloud admin accounts in use

maturity_signals.developing: Least privilege applied to some roles.
maturity_signals.defined: Least privilege enforced; no shared accounts.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Just-enough-access with continuous optimization.

audit_questions: Is hypervisor hardening implemented and verified?
How is tenant isolation technically enforced?
Are container images scanned before deployment?
Is IaC security scanning integrated into the deployment pipeline?
suggested_evidence: Hypervisor hardening guide and compliance scan
Tenant isolation test results or penetration test findings
Container image scan reports from CI/CD pipeline
IaC security scan tool configuration and reports
indicators_of_implementation: Hypervisor configuration matches hardening baseline
No cross-tenant access demonstrated in tests
All images scanned before production deployment
IaC scan failures block deployment
red_flags: Default hypervisor configuration in use
Cross-tenant data accessible in penetration tests
Container images deployed without security scanning
IaC deployed without security review

maturity_signals.defined: Documented hardening; automated scanning; isolation testing.
maturity_signals.managed: Comprehensive scanning in CI/CD; micro-segmentation enforced.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Zero-trust workload security; continuous compliance scanning; automated remediation.

audit_questions: Is hypervisor configuration validated against a hardening guide?
suggested_evidence: Compliance scan report vs hardening baseline
indicators_of_implementation: All hardening checks pass
red_flags: Default or unreviewed hypervisor configuration

audit_questions: Is tenant environment isolation technically enforced?
suggested_evidence: Isolation architecture diagram; penetration test results confirming isolation
indicators_of_implementation: No cross-tenant data leakage in tests
red_flags: Cross-tenant access possible

audit_questions: Is VM lifecycle (provisioning, migration, decommissioning) managed securely?
suggested_evidence: VM lifecycle policy; decommissioning checklist
indicators_of_implementation: Data wiped before decommission; migrations encrypted
red_flags: VMs decommissioned without data sanitization

audit_questions: Are container images scanned and orchestration platforms hardened?
suggested_evidence: Image scanning tool output; Kubernetes CIS benchmark results
indicators_of_implementation: No critical vulnerabilities in deployed images
red_flags: Unscanned images deployed to production

audit_questions: Is micro-segmentation implemented to isolate workloads?
suggested_evidence: Network segmentation architecture; firewall rules
indicators_of_implementation: Workloads cannot communicate laterally without explicit rule
red_flags: Flat network with no workload segmentation

audit_questions: Does the API gateway implement authentication, authorization, and rate limiting?
suggested_evidence: API gateway configuration; security policy documentation
indicators_of_implementation: All API calls authenticated; rate limits enforced
red_flags: Unauthenticated API endpoints exposed

audit_questions: Are IaC templates security scanned before deployment?
suggested_evidence: IaC scan tool (e.g., Checkov, tfsec) integration in CI/CD; scan reports
indicators_of_implementation: Security issues in IaC block deployment pipeline
red_flags: IaC deployed without security scanning

audit_questions: Is cloud storage encrypted server-side with appropriate access controls?
suggested_evidence: Storage encryption configuration; access control policy
indicators_of_implementation: All storage buckets encrypted; public access disabled
red_flags: Unencrypted storage or publicly accessible buckets

audit_questions: Are regular security baseline compliance assessments conducted for all cloud infrastructure?
suggested_evidence: Periodic compliance scan reports against baselines
indicators_of_implementation: Assessments on schedule; deviations remediated
red_flags: No baseline assessment conducted

audit_questions: Is immutable infrastructure implemented for production workloads?
suggested_evidence: IaC deployment policy prohibiting in-place changes; deployment pipeline configuration
indicators_of_implementation: Production changes made by redeploy, not patching in place
red_flags: Mutable production infrastructure with manual changes

audit_questions: Are security baselines documented for all cloud infrastructure types?
Is baseline compliance verified periodically?
suggested_evidence: Security baseline documents per infrastructure type
Periodic compliance scan reports
indicators_of_implementation: Baselines cover all infrastructure categories
Non-compliance alerts are actioned
red_flags: Baselines not documented
Non-compliance drifts unchecked

maturity_signals.defined: Documented baselines; periodic scanning.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Policy-as-code with automatic remediation.

audit_questions: Does the CST apply CSP security recommendations and NCA controls to cloud workloads?
suggested_evidence: Hardened VM configurations; security baseline compliance report
indicators_of_implementation: Workloads pass CSP security posture assessment
red_flags: Default configurations in use; no hardening applied

maturity_signals.defined: Hardening aligned to CSP and NCA guidance.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Continuous security posture management.

audit_questions: Are approved security baselines applied to all cloud-hosted VMs and containers?
suggested_evidence: Compliance scan against baseline for cloud workloads
indicators_of_implementation: All workloads pass baseline scan
red_flags: Workloads with unreviewed configurations

audit_questions: Is workload isolation and network segmentation implemented for cloud applications?
suggested_evidence: Virtual network segmentation configuration
indicators_of_implementation: Applications isolated in separate network segments
red_flags: All applications on flat network

audit_questions: Is access to cloud management interfaces restricted to approved IP addresses or VPN?
Are management protocols encrypted?
suggested_evidence: Network access control configuration restricting management access
TLS/SSH configuration for management protocols
indicators_of_implementation: Management interfaces not accessible from the internet without VPN
No unencrypted management protocols in use
red_flags: Cloud management console accessible from any IP
Unencrypted protocols (HTTP, Telnet) used for management

maturity_signals.defined: IP allowlisting and encrypted protocols enforced.
maturity_signals.managed: VPN-only access with device health checks.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Zero-trust network access with continuous verification.

audit_questions: Is DDoS protection enabled for all cloud-hosted services?
Is network traffic monitored for anomalies?
Are tenant network environments isolated?
suggested_evidence: DDoS protection service configuration
Network monitoring/SIEM alerts and dashboards
Virtual network segmentation architecture diagram
indicators_of_implementation: DDoS protection active on all public endpoints
Network anomaly alerts configured and tested
Tenant networks logically isolated
red_flags: DDoS protection not enabled
No network traffic monitoring
Tenants share network segments

maturity_signals.defined: DDoS protection and network monitoring active.
maturity_signals.optimizing: AI-driven threat detection with automated mitigation.

audit_questions: Are virtual networks segmented and isolated per tenant?
suggested_evidence: VPC/VNET configuration showing tenant isolation
indicators_of_implementation: Each tenant in isolated network segment
red_flags: Shared flat network across tenants

audit_questions: Is DDoS protection enabled on all public-facing cloud services?
suggested_evidence: DDoS mitigation service activation records
indicators_of_implementation: DDoS protection confirmed active
red_flags: Public services without DDoS protection

audit_questions: Is network traffic monitored across all cloud tiers with anomaly detection?
suggested_evidence: Network monitoring tool configuration; alert history
indicators_of_implementation: Alerts generated and responded to for anomalous traffic
red_flags: No network monitoring or alerts

audit_questions: Is a WAF deployed for cloud-hosted web services?
Are IDS/IPS systems active in the cloud environment?
suggested_evidence: WAF configuration and block rule reports
IDS/IPS deployment architecture and alert logs
indicators_of_implementation: WAF active with blocking enabled
IDS/IPS alerts reviewed and responded to
red_flags: No WAF for public web services
IDS/IPS in monitoring-only mode without response

maturity_signals.defined: WAF and IDS/IPS deployed and configured.
maturity_signals.managed: WAF rules tuned; automated incident response.
maturity_signals.optimizing: ML-powered WAF with zero-day protection.

audit_questions: Are virtual firewalls and network ACLs implemented for cloud-hosted workloads?
Is all data in transit encrypted?
suggested_evidence: Virtual firewall and ACL configuration
TLS configuration evidence; network traffic encryption scan
indicators_of_implementation: All traffic between cloud services encrypted
Firewall rules reviewed and approved
red_flags: Unencrypted inter-service communication
Overly permissive firewall rules

maturity_signals.defined: Virtual firewall and TLS enforced.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Zero-trust microsegmentation.

audit_questions: Are virtual firewalls and ACLs configured for all cloud workloads?
suggested_evidence: Firewall and ACL configuration exports
indicators_of_implementation: All workloads behind firewall with deny-default rule
red_flags: No firewall; any-any rules

red_flags: HTTP or TLS 1.0/1.1 in use

indicators_of_implementation: Alert history shows cloud network events reviewed
red_flags: Cloud network traffic not monitored

indicators_of_implementation: No unprotected management endpoints

red_flags: Testing done manually post-deployment

audit_questions: Are HSMs used for all cryptographic key generation and storage?

red_flags: No DR test in past 12 months

audit_questions: Is geographic redundancy implemented for critical cloud services?
suggested_evidence: Multi-region architecture diagram
Failover configuration and test results
indicators_of_implementation: Critical services replicated to alternate site
Failover tested and within defined RTO
red_flags: Single-region architecture with no geographic redundancy
Failover never tested

maturity_signals.developing: Backup in place but no live replication.
maturity_signals.defined: Geographic redundancy; annual failover test.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Active-active multi-region with continuous replication testing.

audit_questions: Do SLAs with tenants document the CSP's BCP/DR capabilities and availability guarantees?
suggested_evidence: SLA documents referencing BCP/DR capabilities
Service availability metrics vs SLA commitments
indicators_of_implementation: SLAs include availability and recovery guarantees
Availability metrics transparently reported
red_flags: SLAs do not address BCP/DR
Availability metrics not disclosed to tenants

maturity_signals.defined: BCP/DR capabilities in SLA.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Tenant-accessible real-time availability dashboard.

audit_questions: Are cloud services included in the entity's BCP/DRP?
Are data backup and restore procedures for cloud-hosted data tested?
Is a cloud service exit plan documented?
suggested_evidence: BCP/DRP document including cloud services section
Backup and restore test records
Cloud exit plan document
indicators_of_implementation: Cloud dependencies identified in BCP
Restore tested successfully
Exit plan documented and reviewed
red_flags: BCP does not cover cloud service failures
Backup not tested
No exit plan

maturity_signals.defined: Cloud in BCP/DRP; backup tested; exit plan documented.
maturity_signals.managed: Cloud failover tested; exit plan rehearsed.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Cloud resilience continuously tested; automated failover.

audit_questions: Are recovery procedures for cloud-dependent business processes documented in BCP?
suggested_evidence: BCP section on cloud-dependent processes with recovery steps
indicators_of_implementation: Cloud dependencies explicitly mapped in BCP
red_flags: BCP does not address cloud service unavailability

audit_questions: Are cloud data backup and restore procedures tested?
suggested_evidence: Backup test records with restore verification
indicators_of_implementation: Restore test completed and within RPO
red_flags: Backups not tested; restore untried

audit_questions: Is a cloud service exit plan documented with data portability procedures?
suggested_evidence: Cloud exit plan document; data portability test
indicators_of_implementation: Exit plan covers data migration and service continuity
red_flags: No exit plan; vendor lock-in risk

audit_questions: Is there a process for security assessing critical cloud suppliers before engagement?
Do supplier contracts include cybersecurity requirements?
Is supplier security posture monitored on an ongoing basis?
suggested_evidence: Supplier security assessment questionnaire and results
Sample supplier contracts with security clauses
Supplier risk register and ongoing monitoring records
indicators_of_implementation: All critical suppliers assessed before engagement
Security clauses in all supplier contracts
Supplier risk monitored at least annually
red_flags: Critical suppliers engaged without security assessment
Contracts missing security requirements
No ongoing supplier monitoring

maturity_signals.defined: Pre-engagement assessment; security clauses in contracts; annual review.
maturity_signals.managed: Continuous supplier risk monitoring; automated alerts.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Integrated supplier risk platform with real-time threat intelligence.

audit_questions: Are critical cloud infrastructure suppliers assessed before engagement?
suggested_evidence: Pre-engagement security assessment reports for critical suppliers
indicators_of_implementation: Assessment required before contract signature
red_flags: Suppliers engaged without assessment

audit_questions: Do all supplier contracts include cybersecurity requirements?
suggested_evidence: Sample supplier contracts with security clauses
indicators_of_implementation: All contracts have security requirements and audit rights
red_flags: Contracts missing security clauses or audit rights

audit_questions: Is supplier cybersecurity posture monitored on an ongoing basis?
suggested_evidence: Supplier risk register with ongoing monitoring records
indicators_of_implementation: Supplier risks reviewed at least annually
red_flags: No ongoing supplier monitoring

audit_questions: Do supplier contracts require incident notification within defined timeframes?
suggested_evidence: Supplier contract incident notification clause
indicators_of_implementation: Notification timeline specified and enforced
red_flags: No incident notification requirement in supplier contracts

audit_questions: Is a critical supplier register maintained with annual risk assessments?
suggested_evidence: Supplier register with risk ratings and last assessment dates
Annual assessment reports for critical suppliers
indicators_of_implementation: Register is current; all critical suppliers assessed in past 12 months
red_flags: Supplier register incomplete or not maintained
Critical suppliers not assessed in past 12 months

maturity_signals.defined: Supplier register with annual assessments.
maturity_signals.managed: Risk-based supplier tiering; automated assessment triggers.
maturity_signals.optimizing: Continuous supplier risk monitoring with automated alerts on posture changes.
